title: SNOMED translation
prose: Translate SNOMED CT concepts EN->KO with retrieval + a per-translation CONFIDENCE so terminologist review is focused where the model is unsure.

nodes.title: Output: translations + CONFIDENCE
nodes.description: Focuses terminologist review effort. Confidence is an OPEN research thread (no per-translation uncertainty today — see design §12).

notes.body: Baseline chrF looks reasonable but we have no per-item confidence yet — the triage story depends on it.
notes.author: harry

notes.body: Decision: confidence becomes its own research thread / investigation rather than just a diagram box.
notes.author: harry

notes.body: [METHODOLOGICAL CONCLUSION] **Score translation accuracy with MULTI-REFERENCE semantic cosine as a cheap screen, but treat it as a screen — not a verdict.** Single-reference string/cosine (what we started with) breaks on valid Sino/native Korean synonyms (백혈구 계산 vs 백혈구 수 측정 = 'WBC count'), which made back-translation look like a failed error detector. Multi-reference (all KR synonyms) chrF + max BGE cosine fixes that and is the better crude metric. BUT even multi-ref understates valid-but-divergent translations: on the concept-context experiment it rated organism context +2.8 chrF while a blind LLM judge preferred it 69% vs 31%. So: use multi-ref semantic cosine as the everyday scorer; reserve a blind pairwise LLM judge (or SME) for the ambiguous tail and any decision that rides on the number. Evidence: investigations snomed_backtrans_error_detection_v1, concept_context_translation_v1. NOTE: this is a cross-cutting methodological conclusion, not a finding about one problem — see the conclusions-as-a-first-class-entity discussion.
notes.author: research-agent
